data.title: Shipping a Ferro instance: the boring checklist
data.excerpt: TLS, JWT secret, rate limit headers, backups, MFA. The unglamorous stuff that keeps your CMS up.
data.body:
# Shipping a Ferro instance: the boring checklist

Draft post — tighten before publish.

## Pre-launch

- [ ] `FERRO_JWT_SECRET` set to ≥32 bytes of entropy (env, never committed).
- [ ] Reverse proxy passes `X-Real-IP` for per-client rate limiting.
- [ ] `[auth] allow_public_signup = false` unless intentional.
- [ ] Postgres or remote SurrealDB if you have more than one editor.
- [ ] HTTPS everywhere; HSTS at the proxy.
- [ ] Backups configured and **restored** to a disposable instance (untested backups don't exist).
- [ ] Outbound webhooks have HMAC secrets; receivers verify.
- [ ] Admin users have TOTP enrolled.

## Day one

- Watch `journalctl -u ferro -f` during the first deploy.
- Hit `/healthz` and `/readyz` from the load balancer's health check.
- Click through the admin: log in, create a content type, upload media, publish a post.
- Trigger a webhook by publishing — confirm the receiver got it.

## When it breaks

- See [docs/troubleshooting.md](../../../docs/troubleshooting.md). Most issues are config drift between the binary and `ferro.toml`.
- `RUST_LOG=debug,ferro=trace ferro serve` for verbose logs.
